seo.title: MATIC/USDT Funding Rates on dYdX DEX - 1h Intervals
seo.description: Monitor MATIC/USDT perpetual futures funding rates on dYdX with hourly intervals. Track arbitrage opportunities on the Cosmos-based DEX platform.

content.intro: dYdX offers MATIC/USDT perpetual futures on its Cosmos-based appchain with hourly funding rate settlements. The exchange's transition from StarkEx to its own blockchain provides sovereignty while maintaining decentralized perpetual trading for Polygon's native token.
content.how_funding_works: dYdX calculates funding rates hourly using a time-weighted average price (TWAP) mechanism comparing the perpetual price to the underlying index. Funding payments occur every hour at the top of each hour, with rates determined by the premium/discount of the perpetual contract relative to the spot price. The exchange uses a dampening factor to prevent excessive rate volatility, and traders pay or receive funding based on their position size and the prevailing rate.
content.why_this_pair: MATIC/USDT on dYdX presents unique arbitrage opportunities due to Polygon's ecosystem growth and staking dynamics affecting spot market liquidity. The hourly funding frequency allows for more granular rate capture compared to 8-hour cycles on centralized exchanges, while the pair's correlation with Ethereum ecosystem developments creates predictable funding patterns.

sections.heading: Historical MATIC Funding Rate Patterns
sections.body: MATIC funding rates on dYdX typically range from -0.01% to 0.05% hourly, with significant spikes during Polygon network upgrades or zkEVM announcements. The token exhibits strong correlation with Ethereum gas prices, often showing negative funding when Layer 2 adoption increases. Seasonal patterns emerge around quarterly unlock events and validator staking rewards distribution. The hourly settlement creates more opportunities to capture short-term rate anomalies compared to traditional 8-hour cycles.

sections.heading: Arbitrage Opportunities and Execution
sections.body: MATIC funding arbitrage on dYdX benefits from the platform's 0.02% maker fees and hourly rate resets. Cross-exchange arbitrage becomes profitable when dYdX rates diverge more than 0.05% from Binance or OKX 8-hour averages. The Cosmos appchain architecture enables faster settlement but requires careful gas management for USDC deposits. Automated strategies should account for the 1-hour funding frequency and potential slippage during high-volatility periods when MATIC correlates strongly with broader crypto market moves.

sections.heading: Risk Considerations and Position Management
sections.body: MATIC's correlation with Ethereum ecosystem developments creates systematic risk during network upgrades or competitive L2 launches. The token's 50% supply held by insiders presents concentration risk during major unlocks. dYdX's lower post-migration volumes can amplify slippage costs during volatile periods, particularly affecting larger arbitrage positions. Traders should monitor Polygon validator performance and staking yields, as network issues can cause rapid funding rate shifts and impact the reliability of arbitrage strategies.

faq.question: How often are MATIC funding rates calculated on dYdX?
faq.answer: dYdX calculates and settles MATIC funding rates every hour on the hour. This provides more frequent settlement compared to most centralized exchanges that use 8-hour cycles, creating additional arbitrage opportunities for active traders.

faq.question: What affects MATIC funding rates on dYdX specifically?
faq.answer: MATIC funding rates are influenced by the perpetual-to-spot price premium, Polygon network developments, and Ethereum gas price movements. dYdX's lower liquidity post-Cosmos migration can amplify rate movements during high activity periods or major Polygon ecosystem announcements.

faq.question: Can I earn funding fees by holding MATIC positions on dYdX?
faq.answer: Yes, you receive funding payments when holding positions opposite to the funding direction. If funding is positive, short sellers pay long holders hourly. The frequent 1-hour settlements allow for more granular income generation compared to longer settlement cycles.

faq.question: What are the main risks of MATIC funding arbitrage on dYdX?
faq.answer: Key risks include dYdX's reduced liquidity post-migration creating higher slippage, MATIC's correlation with Ethereum affecting predictability, and the technical complexity of the Cosmos appchain potentially causing settlement delays during network congestion.
